summary: Iter 41 — 6th retest of CapitalDetailModal modal-stays-open bug. Main agent applied (a) removed onInteractOutside/onFocusOutside preventDefault from DialogContent (regression fix), (b) added silent=true parameter to reload() so bulk-delete/single-delete code paths skip setLoading(true). RESULT: BULK DELETE STILL CLOSES MODAL (6th straight failure). SINGLE DELETE STILL CLOSES MODAL. Regression tests PASS (backdrop click, X/Kapat button, Esc key all close the modal correctly). New refined RCA below.

ui_bugs.issue: Modal STILL closes after bulk delete. Pre: ₺1.252.000 (152K cash, 26 movements). Click Sil(26): modal_visible_after = False at every poll (500ms..3000ms). Post: cash ₺145.000 (7 movements actually deleted — the rest are linked to transactions/cars and can't be deleted server-side). The silent-reload fix is INSUFFICIENT because the failure path is not loading=true; it is the OPTIMISTIC setMovements filter. When setMovements removes selected rows AND the result is movements.length === 0, CashTab takes the early-return at line 242-247 ('Henüz nakit hareketi yok') which UNMOUNTS the entire toolbar including the focused bulk-delete-btn. Focus jumps to <body>. Because this fix REMOVED onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()}, Radix's FocusScope detects orphan focus and dispatches onOpenChange(false) → modal closes.

Single-delete (legacy trash icon, selection mode OFF) ALSO still closes the modal. Reproduced first: clicked one [data-testid^='delete-movement-'] → modal_visible_after = False (1 movement deleted, cash 153K→152K). Same root cause: setMovements filter removes the row → trash button unmounts → focus to <body> → modal closes. Even when remaining movements > 0 (so the !movements.length empty state is NOT triggered), the trash button itself is in the deleted row, so it unmounts.

passed_tests: REGRESSION FIX VERIFIED: backdrop click closes modal ✅
REGRESSION FIX VERIFIED: Kapat (X) button closes modal ✅
REGRESSION FIX VERIFIED: Esc key closes modal ✅
failed_tests: BULK DELETE — modal closes (6TH STRAIGHT FAIL). Sil(26) clicked → modal_visible=False from t=500ms. Screenshot: /app/.screenshots/iter41_bulk_after.png
SINGLE DELETE — modal closes after first row deleted (no movements.length=0 condition; trash button itself unmounted in deleted row).
action_items: PRIMARY FIX (RECOMMENDED — addresses the actual focus-loss vector): re-add ONLY onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} to DialogContent (do NOT add onInteractOutside; that one over-broadly blocks backdrop click and was correctly removed). This is the MINIMAL guard against the focus-to-body event that fires when the focused button (trash icon or bulk-delete-btn-in-empty-toolbar) unmounts via setMovements filter. The previous iters that had onFocusOutside ALSO had onInteractOutside which was the regression — onFocusOutside alone preserves backdrop close.

DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH FIX (PREFERRED, structural): refactor CashTab so the toolbar (Seç/Tümünü Seç/Sil/İptal) is ALWAYS rendered, even when movements.length === 0. Current code at lines 242-247 early-returns the empty-state div, discarding the toolbar. Move the empty-state inside the list section: render toolbar first, then if !movements.length show empty-state, else map movements. This keeps bulk-delete-btn mounted and focus stable.

SECONDARY (for single-delete): before calling setMovements filter in handleDeleteMovement, blur the trash button (e.target.blur()) AND immediately programmatically focus a stable element inside the dialog (e.g., the dialog title or the close button). This prevents focus from briefly landing on <body>. Same can be applied to handleBulkDelete: blur bulk-delete-btn and focus a stable element BEFORE setMovements optimistic update.

ALTERNATIVE — if onFocusOutside still doesn't catch it (unlikely), add explicit guard inside Dialog onOpenChange: const closeReasonRef = useRef('user'); pass it down so handlers can set ref='delete' before mutation; in onOpenChange check the ref and ignore close events that originate from delete actions. (More invasive, only if simpler fix fails.)

ALTERNATIVE — skip optimistic setMovements entirely for handleBulkDelete; only rely on the silent reload to refresh the list. This means the rows visually persist for ~200-500ms (until reload returns) but the DOM stays stable. Focus stays on bulk-delete-btn the whole time.
critical_code_review_comments: After 6 iterations the modal-stays-open bug remains unresolved. Pattern: each fix targets ONE focus-loss vector (blur, disabled attr, loading-unmount), but the optimistic-setMovements vector was never directly addressed. The MINIMAL correct fix is onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} alone (without onInteractOutside).

CashTab line 242-247 unconditionally returns an empty-state div when movements.length === 0, discarding the toolbar. This is the structural root of the bulk-delete failure when ALL movements are deleted. Refactor to keep toolbar mounted.

The optimistic update pattern (setMovements before the fetch returns) means the trash button in single-delete is ALWAYS unmounted by the click handler that the user just pressed — focus inevitably lands on <body>. Either delay the optimistic update until after focus has been moved, or guard with onFocusOutside.
Comment at line 90-91 ('selectionMode AÇIK kalıyor — bulk-delete-btn DOM'da kalır') is INCORRECT when all movements are deleted: the empty-state branch unmounts the toolbar entirely.
Triple state refresh (reload + refreshCapital + fetchData) at lines 94-96 is still redundant and may cause unnecessary parent re-renders.
REGRESSION SUITE NOW PASSES: backdrop click, X/Kapat, Esc key all correctly close the modal — keep them passing in the next fix attempt by NOT re-adding onInteractOutside preventDefault.

success_rate.frontend: 60% — 3 regression tests pass; 2 primary tests (bulk + single delete modal-stays-open) still fail for 6th iter

seed_data_creation: Seeded 18 cash deposits via /api/capital/adjust over the run. After bulk delete, 7 movements got deleted (rest are linked to transactions and protected server-side), cash dropped from ₺152K to ₺145K. Re-seed: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST $URL/api/auth/login -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"email":"test_branch_6b287ede@test.com","password":"Password123!"}' | python3 -c 'import sys,json;print(json.load(sys.stdin)["token"]')) ; for i in {1..10}; do curl -s -X POST $URL/api/capital/adjust -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{"type":"deposit","amount":1000,"description":"TEST"}'; done
context_for_next_testing_agent: 6 iterations on this bug. ROOT CAUSE (definitively this iter): the OPTIMISTIC setMovements filter inside handleDeleteMovement/handleBulkDelete unmounts the focused trash button (single delete) OR triggers CashTab's !movements.length early-return that discards the toolbar (bulk delete when all rows deleted). Focus jumps to <body>. Without the onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} guard (removed in iter 41 to fix the backdrop regression), Radix dispatches onOpenChange(false). RECOMMENDED FIX: re-add ONLY onFocusOutside (not onInteractOutside — that's the broad one that blocks backdrop). PLUS keep toolbar always-mounted in CashTab (move !movements.length empty-state INSIDE the list section, not as an early-return). Regression tests (backdrop, X, Esc) currently PASS — preserve that. Test files: none new. Iter 40 report: /app/test_reports/iteration_40.json.
rca_of_the_issue: After 6 iters, the precise sequence: (1) user clicks [data-testid^='delete-movement-X'] (single) or [data-testid='bulk-delete-btn'] (bulk). (2) Click handler runs: setMovements(prev => prev.filter(...)) optimistic update. (3) For single-delete: the deleted row contains the clicked trash button → React unmounts it during the next render. For bulk-delete-all: movements.length becomes 0 → CashTab early-returns empty-state div, discarding the toolbar including bulk-delete-btn. (4) The browser's focused element no longer exists → focus falls to <body>. (5) Radix Dialog's FocusScope detects focus-leave-from-content → fires onFocusOutside event. (6) Iter 41 REMOVED the e.preventDefault() guard on onFocusOutside (to fix the iter 40 backdrop-click regression). (7) Without the guard, Radix dispatches onOpenChange(false) → onClose() → modal closes. FIX: re-add ONLY onFocusOutside={(e)=>e.preventDefault()} (NOT onInteractOutside — that broke backdrop). Optionally combine with structural fix to keep toolbar mounted. Reproduction (deterministic): login → click capital-amount-display → wait for total-capital-amount → if 0 movements, seed via API → click capital-tab-cash → click enter-selection-mode-btn → click toggle-select-all-btn → click bulk-delete-btn → wait 1s → assert is_visible('total-capital-amount') === True (currently False).
